data.title: Lixiang Education regains Nasdaq compliance
data.description:
• Lixiang Education Holding (NASDAQ:LXEH) has notified that the Co. for the last 10 consecutive business days, from Sept. 30 to Oct. 11, 2024, the closing bid price of its ADS has been at $1.00/share or greater.
  • Accordingly, the Co. has regained compliance with Listing Rule 5450(a)(1), and this matter is now closed.

data.title: Google SVP Prabhakar Raghavan, who helms Search and Ads, to become Chief Technologist at the firm
Intel rises even as Goldman Sachs cuts earnings estimates on 'muted' PC demand
data.description:
• Intel (NASDAQ:INTC) shares rose 1.4% in afternoon trading on Thursday, even as Goldman Sachs lowered its earnings estimates on the semiconductor giant, citing "muted" PC demand.
  • "... [W]e reduce our 2025/26 non-GAAP EPS (excl. SBC) estimates by 7% as we reflect a slower recovery in the PC CPU and FPGA businesses, partially offset by a more constructive outlook for server CPUs," analyst Toshiya Hari wrote in a note to clients. "While we believe management is taking the necessary steps to right-size the organization, we remain guarded on the stock as we have yet to see signs that indicate a return to process technology leadership (if anything, Intel may continue to outsource a significant fraction of its client CPU tiles to TSMC beyond 2025, per our checks), and as Intel continues to lack a meaningful presence in the lucrative data center accelerator market."
  • Hari maintained his Sell rating and $21 price target on Intel.
  • A consensus of analysts expect Intel to lose $0.03 per share on $13.04B in revenue for the coming quarter.

data.title: Atlanta Fed raises GDPNow estimate to 3.4% to 3.2%
data.description:
The Federal Reserve Bank of Atlanta on Thursday lifted its GDPNow growth estimate for Q3 to 3.4% from 3.2% last week.

According to the Fed branch, it raised its estimate for real personal consumption expenditures growth in the latest quarter to 3.6% from 3.3%, while its view for real gross private domestic investment growth was cut to 3.1% from 3.3%.

“GDP has been remarkably strong,” Renaissance Macro Research said on X, while raising a “few concerns” going to Q4. “Real PCE will soften given slower income growth,” it said, and pointed to the ongoing Boeing labor strike’s expected impact on equipment investment.

The Atlanta Fed’s move came as the Census Bureau reported retail sales rose 0.4% last month. The print easily topped the Street’s consensus for a 0.3% advance.

“The real upside was in the core measures of spending,” Wells Fargo said in a note. “Control group sales, which tends to line up with personal consumption spending in the GDP report, also came in much stronger than expected with 0.7% gain.”

The Street was expecting a 0.3% gain.

According to the note, the rise in sales excluding car dealerships and gasoline stations, was the largest in three months and the third highest of the year. Also, control group sales have topped the headline result in five of the last seven months, the note added.

More on the US Economy

  • Tracking The Current U.S. Business Cycle In 4 Charts
  • U.S. import and export prices fall more than expected in September
  • Consumers see increased probability of missing debt payments in next 3 months: NY Fed

data.title: Molina, Centene see big drops following Elevance Health profit warning
data.description:
• Molina Healthcare (NYSE:MOH) and Centene (NYSE:CNC) have both seen significant declines in Thursday trading after fellow health insurer Elevance Health (ELV) lowered its full-year earnings forecast.
  • In early afternoon trading, Molina is off ~13%, while Centene is down ~10%. Much of the two companies' revenues comes from providing insurance through Medicaid and Medicare.
  • On Oct. 15, while UnitedHealth Group (UNH) beat Wall Street estimates with its Q3 results, cost concerns in its insurance sent jitters throughout the Street.

• J.B. Hunt Transport Services (NASDAQ:JBHT) declared $0.43/share quarterly dividend, in line with previous.

data.title: BP weighs sale of minority stake in offshore wind business - Reuters
data.description:
BP (NYSE:BP) is considering selling a minority stake in its offshore wind business, in the latest attempt by CEO Murray Auchincloss to trim its focus on renewable energy, Reuters reported Thursday.

BP (BP) is working with Bank of America to find partners for the business, as the company wants to reduce its share of the hefty investments required to develop these projects, although it remains committed to developing its major offshore wind projects, according to the report.

The company does not yet have any offshore wind farms in operation; its offshore business has stakes in projects in the U.S., U.K., Germany, and Asia, and had a pipeline of projects with a capacity of 9.6 GW at the end of June.

BP (BP) paused investments in new offshore wind projects, Reuters reported in June, and last month it announced plans to sell its U.S. onshore wind business.

data.title: OpenAI releases test versions of ChatGPT app for Windows
data.description:
• Microsoft-backed (NASDAQ:MSFT) OpenAI has released early test versions of the ChatGPT Windows desktop app for Plus, Enterprise, Team and Edu users, the company announced today in a post on X.
  • The test version of the Windows app can be downloaded here. It is already fully available for macOS.
  • Team is designed for collaborative workspaces, while Enterprise is designed for larger businesses. ChatGPT Edu provides artificial intelligence tools for students, faculty, researchers and administrators.
  • ChatGPT Plus is a subscription plan that provides several benefits compared to the free version, such as faster response times and the ability to build custom GPTs. It costs $20 per month.

data.title: GameSquare stock rallies on raised FY24 annual revenue view to $105M-$110M
data.description:
• GameSquare Holdings (NASDAQ:GAME) has increased its annual revenue guidance and now expect annual proforma revenue of $105M to $110M vs. consensus of $104.39M for 2024, compared to our prior guidance of $100M.
  • The Co. currently estimates that 2024 Q3 gross margin was ~18%, compared to 14.8% for the Q2 of 2024.
  • Q3 gross margin benefitted from revenue mix, which was partially offset by start-up costs associated with new programs and relationships that are expected to benefit both revenue and profitability in the fourth quarter of 2024.
  • Expected adjusted EBITDA loss of approximately $2.4M, representing a $3M improvement from Q2, 2024.
  • The Co.'s performance during the Q3 2024 demonstrates the progress it is making and the positive momentum underway as it executes against these initiatives.
  • GameSquare will release its final third quarter of 2024 financial results after the market closes on Nov. 14, 2024.
  • GAME shares +35% to 0.95.

data.title: Nestlé cuts full-year outlook amid weak consumer demand
data.description:
Nestlé (OTCPK:NSRGY) reported a 2.4% year-on-year decline in total sales for the first nine months of 2024.

Sales amounted to CHF 67.1B vs. CHF 68.8B in the same period last year. Foreign exchange reduced sales by 4.1% and net divestitures by 0.3%.

Organic growth was 2.0% for the first nine months, with positive real internal growth (RIG). RIG was 0.5%, impacted by soft consumer demand and consumer hesitancy towards global brands, linked to geopolitical tension.

“We delivered organic sales growth, driven by positive real internal growth. Consumer demand has weakened in recent months, and we expect the demand environment to remain soft. Given this outlook and our further actions to reduce customer inventories in the fourth quarter, we have updated our full-year guidance," said CEO Laurent Freixe.

Full-year 2024 outlook updated: Organic sales growth is now expected to be around 2% (compared to prior expectation of at least 3% growth), in line with the first nine months. Underlying trading operating profit margin is expected to be at around 17.0%. Underlying earnings per share growth in constant currency is expected to be broadly flat (compared to prior expectation of mid single-digit growth).
